::::{#exm-fubini-nonrect}

#### Changing the order of integration for a non-rectangular region

Adapted from [@larsonCalc11e, Section 14.2, Example 4, pp. 984--985].

Let $X$ and $Y$ be independent $\operatorname{Uniform}(0, 1)$ random variables,
with joint density $f(x, y) = 1$ on the unit square $[0, 1]^2$.
Compute the expectation $\E{\ef{-X^2}\,\indicp{Y \le X}}$.

Because the joint density equals $1$ on $[0, 1]^2$,
this expectation is the double integral of $\ef{-x^2}$
over the triangular region where $Y \le X$,
namely $D = \{(x, y) : 0 \le x \le 1,\; 0 \le y \le x\}$:

$$\E{\ef{-X^2}\,\indicp{Y \le X}} = \iint_D \ef{-x^2}\,dA.$$

**Order $dx\,dy$ is intractable.** Re-describing $D$ as
$D = \{(x, y) : 0 \le y \le 1,\; y \le x \le 1\}$, the inner integral is

$$\int_y^1 \ef{-x^2}\,dx,$$

which has no elementary antiderivative.

**Order $dy\,dx$ works.** Applying @thm-fubini Part 1
($\ef{-x^2}$ is continuous and $D$ is the vertically simple region
$0 \le x \le 1$, $0 \le y \le x$):

$$
\ba
\iint_D \ef{-x^2}\,dA
&= \int_0^1\!\int_0^x \ef{-x^2}\,dy\,dx
\\&= \int_0^1 \ef{-x^2}\paren{\int_0^x dy}\,dx
\\&= \int_0^1 x\,\ef{-x^2}\,dx
\\&= \sb{-\frac{1}{2}\,\ef{-x^2}}_0^1
\\&= -\frac{1}{2}\paren{\ef{-1} - 1}
\\&= \frac{e - 1}{2e}
\\&\approx 0.316
\ea
$$

:::{#fig-fubini-nonrect}

```{r}
#| code-fold: true
#| message: false

n_grid <- 51
x_seq <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_grid)
y_seq <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_grid)

z_mat <- outer(x_seq, y_seq, function(x, y) {
  z <- exp(-x^2)
  z[y > x] <- NA
  z
})

plotly::plot_ly(x = ~x_seq, y = ~y_seq, z = ~t(z_mat)) |>
  plotly::add_surface(showscale = FALSE) |>
  plotly::layout(scene = list(
    xaxis = list(title = "x"),
    yaxis = list(title = "y"),
    zaxis = list(title = "z = exp(-x^2)"),
    camera = list(eye = list(x = 1.6, y = -1.6, z = 0.8))
  ))
```

Surface $z = e^{-x^2}$ over the triangular region $0 \le y \le x \le 1$.
The surface depends only on $x$ (constant in $y$), so for each $x$ the
inner integral over $y \in [0, x]$ contributes $x \cdot e^{-x^2}$.

:::

::::
